D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Spatial Frequency & Image Sums

build a pattern by stacking sinusoidal gratings of rising spatial frequency

A square bar pattern is the sum of a fundamental grating plus its odd harmonics. Each added higher frequency sharpens the edges — but a real lens passes only frequencies inside its aperture.

Add harmonics to approach a sharp square grating, and set the base spatial frequency. The strip is the reconstructed image; the bars show which spatial frequencies are present.
